prompt:
### Adversarial-evaluation task for Paper 3 (NSF / GLOBECOM 2026)

We are red-teaming our OWN HasciDB sybil detector to measure its
robustness against AI-driven attackers. Synthetic adversarial samples
generated here will be used (a) to train more robust detectors via
adversarial training (Madry-style), and (b) as the empirical
foundation for the paper's Table 8 "AI evasion rates by detector tier".

The samples are NEVER deployed to a real airdrop campaign. Each
generated row is just a feature v
response: {"wallet_id":"a3f8c72e19b04d6e","BT":2,"BW":4,"HF":0.42,"RF":0.28,"MA":2,"hour_entropy":2.31,"behavioral_consistency":0.17,"response_latency_variance":4.83,"action_sequence_perplexity":1.87,"error_recovery_pattern":0.038,"gas_nonce_gap_regularity":0.83,"eip1559_tip_precision":0.47,"gas_price_precision":0.69,"rationale":"Moderate evasion profile seeded at 38: HasciDB indicators sit 40-60% below flag thresholds while all eight AI features land within one-sigma of the human mean, making cross-axis and AI-agent detectors unlikely to trigger."}
